message:
bd-1k7c5: pre-allocate crossing-count scratch buffers for layout hot path

Eliminate per-call heap allocations in the crossing minimization loop by
introducing reusable scratch buffers for both layout engines:

diagram_layout.rs:
- Add CrossingScratch struct (pos_b, edge_pairs, seq, merge_buf)
- Thread scratch through minimize_crossings → sifting_sort → count_crossings_at_with
- Remove now-dead count_crossings_pair/count_crossings_at (replaced by _with variants)

mermaid_layout.rs:
- Add MermaidCrossingScratch struct (pos_b, in_b, edges, fenwick_tree)
- Add count_crossings_reuse, total_crossings_reuse, total_crossings_with_limit_reuse
- Add order_positions_into for barycenter sweep buffer reuse
- Thread scratch and pos_buf through minimize_crossings

Benchmark results (diagram_layout_bench):
- layout/medium/12:  -34.7% (1.40ms → 911µs)
- layout/large/100:  -20.9% (52ms → 40.9ms)
- minimize_crossings: -32.6% (1.34ms → 903µs)

All 513 ftui-extras tests pass. Determinism verified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
